summary: Skipped next-anchor-only legacy mark-scheme pages and clamped padded crop bottoms so following-page question starts do not enter the active crop.
test_results: Focused loop verification passed in the final session: 111 focused tests passed and 12 rendering-marker tests passed.
acceptance_criteria_status: Satisfied: next-anchor-only following pages are skipped for the active legacy question.
Satisfied: padded mark-scheme crop bottoms are clamped to the computed boundary.
Satisfied: the S20 P51 q6 targeted evidence excludes q7 contamination.
repo_hygiene_notes: No generated outputs or canonical inputs were changed.
known_risks: The heuristic is intentionally conservative but still needs corpus-level auditing for valid continuation-page edge cases.
implementation_summary: Added _next_anchor_page_has_no_legacy_continuation to detect following pages that contain only a later legacy anchor.
Used that helper in _blocks_for_legacy_anchor_bounds so next-anchor-only pages are skipped.
Used the same check in _mark_scheme_regions_for_start for rendered regions.
Clamped padded mark-scheme crop bottoms to the computed boundary so padding cannot pull in the next question.
Added a regression test for a near-top next anchor on the following page.
behavior_change: Legacy mark-scheme crops now exclude a following page that starts the next question without carrying active-question continuation content.
scope_control: The crop-padding clamp is only applied to the computed legacy mark-scheme region bottom.
